tags.description: Everything about read and write OpcUa Attributes

tags.description: Combine multiple requests in one HTTP call

get.description: Only available as Websocket, so you have to make an HTTP Upgrade request.

200.description: StatusChangedEvent: New Status of OpcUa Session

get.description:
Authenticate web session.
* If there is no web session with provided clientId, a new web session will be created.  
* When called with HTTP Basic Auth, the session will be created with the provided credentials. If the credentials are invalid, the session will be created without an user.  
* When called without HTTP Basic Auth, the session will be created without an user.

200.description:
Successfully authenticated.  
The session ID is returned in a cookie named `ClientId`. This cookie needs to be included in subsequent requests.
If username is missing in the response, the session was created without an user because no credentials were provided.

401.description:
Unauthorized. The provided credentials are invalid or login has timed out.  
The session ID is returned in a cookie named 'ClientId'. You need to include this cookie in subsequent requests.  
Check the AR logger for further information.

500.description:
Internal server error.  
The session could not be created. This should not happen.

get.description:
This request resets the timeout which destroys the client session (created with auth request) after 60s without any request. 
The session will be destroyed if not used for 60s (no requests, no websocket connection).

If there is an active websocket connection, it is not neccessary to use this request. 
The session will be kept alive as long as the websocket connection is active.

Note that this session has nothing todo with the opcua session. Its the general client session which may contain multiple opcua sessions.
The opcua session has its own timeout which can be configured with the timeout parameter in the opcua session request.
Reads the IPv4 address on which the server can reach himself. On ARemb this returns 127.0.0.1; On ARsim targets, this handler returns the IP address of the interface the ARsim is bound to.

post.description:
Creates a new opcua session, establish a connection to OPC UA server.

Calling this function successfully creates a secure channel to the server endpoint and it creates 
and activates an application session in the server application.
If the connection was lost the session will be reconnected.
If the server was restarted the session will be recreated.

Without any request, the session is automatically disconnect and deleted after a configurable timeout period.
This prevents any resource leaks on the server side where dead sessions are kept alive forever.
The timeout time can be configured in milliseconds with the timeout parameter.
The default value is 30 seconds and with zero the timeout is disabled.
To reset the timeout cyclically, use the HEAD request. An interval of timeout/2 is recommended.

get.description: Fetch session info with specified id.

delete.description:
Delete session with specified id.

Calling this closes the session and the secure channel connection to the server.
The subscriptions assigned to the session are also deleted.
After delete is called, no more events will be sent for this session object and related subscription objects. 
The disconnect blocks until all outstanding service calls are finished and results are returned.

head.description:
Reset session timeout with specified id.

When creating a new opcua session with /opcoa/sessions PATCH request, a timeout in milliseconds can be configured.
With this HEAD request as with all other /opcua/sessions/ requests the timer for the timeout is reseted.
If the timer expires the opcua session gets deleted. Sending this request in an interval of timeout/2 is recommended.

patch.description:
Change user for the session.

Changing the user identity for a Session may cause 
discontinuities in active Subscriptions because 
the Server may have to tear down connections to an underlying 
system and re-establish them using the new credentials.
In general subscriptions are retained. Events for changed permissions are sent for
subscribed items.

put.description: change value of opcua node attribute

parameters.name: methodId
parameters.description: Id of the method to call.

get.description: Read opcua node references. Browse OPC UA server address space with one starting node. The Service is used by a client to navigate through the Address Space by passing a starting Node and direction.  The server returns the list of referenced nodes matching the criteria.

parameters.name: browseDirection
parameters.description: An enumeration that specifies the direction of reverences to follow.

get.description: request the Server to translate a browse paths to a NodeId for the target node.

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: The RelativePath contains a sequence of BrowseNames

201.description: status and id of created subscription

revisedSamplingInterval.description:
The actual sampling interval that the Server will use.
This value is based on a number of factors, including capabilities of the underlying system. The Server shall always return a revisedSamplingInterval that is equal or higher than the requested samplingInterval. If the requested samplingInterval is higher than the maximum sampling interval supported by the Server, the maximum sampling interval is returned.

revisedQueueSize.description: The actual queue size that the Server will use.

patch.description: Modify settings of monitored item. See request body section for possible settings.

delete.description: Delete monitored item with specified id

post.description:
Combine multiple requests with same starting path segments by using [JSON batching](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/json-batching).
The main goal is to reduce number of requests to the webserver and opc ua server. As performance optimization requests may be combined into one service call to opcua server if possible (i.e multiple GET value requests always handled as one). The order in which the requests are handled is not defined!

The example shows how a GET and PUT method can be combined for two different node.

If the request contains a "body" the "Content-Type" has to be definded in "headers" object of the request (see example below)

get.description: Get ID of the default (System) language.

Get all texts of specified language ID and namespace.
### Authentication:
Authentication is required for all namespaces except `BR` and its sub-namespaces. Authentication is done by calling the `/auth` endpoint.

200.description: Array of text objects (text key + text value). If a text is not available in the requested language, the text of the fallback language is returned.

get.description:
Get a text of a textkey of a specified language ID and namespace.  
### Authentication:
Authentication is required for all namespaces except `BR` and its sub-namespaces. Authentication is done by calling the `/auth` endpoint.

200.description: One single text object (text key + text value) of the requested language. If text is not available in the requested language, the text of the fallback language is returned.

Combine multiple requests with same starting path segments by using [JSON batching](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/json-batching).

MessageSecurityMode.description:
Selection of the OPC UA security mode.

Defines the security policies with which messages are transferred between the OPC UA server and client. If best available is selected as the message security policy, the OPC UA server is queried as to which is available and the best policy is applied.

SecurityPolicy.description:
Selection of the cryptographic algorithm.

Selects the encryption algorithm (security policy) with which the signature and messages should be encrypted during communication between the OPC UA server and client. If best available is selected as the security policy, the OPC UA server is queried as to which is available and the best policy is applied.

SslConfiguration.description:
Select SSL configuration.

Selects the SSL configuration in which the certificate and key to be used are entered.
If no SSL configuration is specified, a default certificate and default key are created and used.

UaStatus.description: Statuscode as descriped in the OPC-UA standard. Currently it can also returns the Status "GoodPasswordChangeRequired" with value 0x00EF0000. This Status will be Part of the standard in the future.

AnyValue.description: Can be any value - string, number, boolean, array or object.

ExpandedNodeId.description: An expanded node id is a node id where the namespace index can be given as index or as a string URI

nodeId.description: If namespaceUri is specified, the namespace index is invalid.

namespaceUri.description: If this value is specified, the namespace index in nodeId is 0 and must be ignored.

serverIndex.description: Index that identifies the Server that contains the TargetNode.  This Server may be the local Server or a remote Server. This index is the index of that Server in the local Server’s Server table.  The index of the local Server in the Server table is always 0.  All remote Servers have indexes greater than 0.

BrowsePathTargets.description: All result target nodes for the browse path.

clientHandle.description: Client-supplied id of the MonitoredItem. This id is used in Notifications generated for the list Node.

samplingInterval.description: The interval that defines the fastest rate at which the MonitoredItem(s) should be accessed and evaluated. This interval is defined in milliseconds. The value 0 indicates that the Server should use the fastest practical rate. The value -1 indicates that the default sampling interval defined by the publishing interval of the Subscription is requested. A different sampling interval is used if the publishing interval is not a supported sampling interval. Any negative number is interpreted as -1. The sampling interval is not changed if the publishing interval is changed by a subsequent call to the ModifySubscription Service. The Server uses this parameter to assign the MonitoredItems to a sampling interval that it supports. The assigned interval is provided in the revisedSamplingInterval parameter. The Server shall always return a          revisedSamplingInterval that is equal or higher than the requested samplingInterval. If the requested          samplingInterval is higher than the maximum sampling interval supported by the Server, the maximum sampling            interval is returned.

queueSize.description:
The requested size of the MonitoredItem queue.
The following values have special meaning for data monitored items:
| Value | Meaning | | ------- | -------- | | 0 or 1 | the Server returns the default queue size which shall be 1 as revisedQueueSize for data monitored items. The queue has a single entry, effectively disabling queuing. | For values larger than one a first-in-first-out queue is to be used. The Server may limit the size in revisedQueueSize. In the case of a queue overflow, the Overflow bit (flag) in the InfoBits portion of the DataValue statusCode is set in the new value.

The following values have special meaning for event monitored items:
| Value | Meaning | | ------- | -------- | | 0 | the Server returns the default queue size for Event Notifications as revisedQueueSize for event monitored items. | | 1 | the Server returns the minimum queue size the Server requires for Event Notifications as revisedQueueSize. | | MaxUInt32 | the Server returns the maximum queue size that the Server can support for Event Notifications as revisedQueueSize. | If a Client chooses a value between the minimum and maximum settings of the Server the value shall be returned in the revisedQueueSize. If the requested queueSize is outside the minimum or maximum, the Server shall return the corresponding bounding value. In the case of a queue overflow, an Event of the type EventQueueOverflowEventType is generated.

MonitoringMode.description:
The monitoringMode is an enumeration that specifies whether sampling and reporting are enabled or disabled for a MonitoredItem. This paramter is helpful to reduce performance overhead for not used monitored items but still keep them for next use. When a MonitoredItem is enabled (i.e. when the monitoringMode is changed from 'disabled' to 'sampling' or 'reporting'), the Server shall report the first sample as soon as possible and the time of this sample becomes the starting point for the next sampling interval. Setting the mode to 'disabled' causes all queued notifications to be deleted.

disabled - The item being monitored is not sampled or evaluated, and Notifications are not generated or queued. Notification reporting is disabled.
sampling - The item being monitored is sampled and evaluated, and Notifications are generated and queued. Notification reporting is disabled.
reporting - (default) The item being monitored is sampled and evaluated, and Notifications are generated and queued. Notification reporting is enabled.

nodeId.name: nodeId
nodeId.description: Id of the node. The node id is splitted into namespace index (ns) and identifier type plus identifier. Valid identifier types are integer (i) and string (s). Sample: ns=10;i=1234

attributeName.name: attributeName
attributeName.description: Name of attribute to be read

NotFound.description: The specified resource was not found

TooManyMatches.description: The requested operation has too many matches to return

ServiceUnavailable.description: Service is not available on your system
